message.content:
# OSS KB Update Proposal

## 1. Pages Meriting Updates

- **postcss** — Yes, update required. New advisory with distinct CVE and fix version.
- **basic-ftp** — Yes, update required. New advisory with distinct fix version.
- **debug** — No update required (see §4 below).

---

## 2. New Known Vulnerabilities Rows

### postcss

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **CVE / GHSA** | CVE-2026-41305 / GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 |
| **Severity** | Moderate |
| **Summary** | XSS via unescaped `</style>` sequences in CSS stringify output. When user-submitted CSS is parsed and re-stringified for embedding in HTML `<style>` tags, a `</style>` payload in CSS values breaks out of the style context, enabling script injection. |
| **Affected versions** | All versions prior to 8.5.10 |
| **Fixed version** | 8.5.10 |
| **References** | [GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93](https://github.com/postcss/postcss/security/advisories/GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93), [NVD](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41305), [Release 8.5.10](https://github.com/postcss/postcss/releases/tag/8.5.10) |

### basic-ftp

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **CVE / GHSA** | GHSA-rp42-5vxx-qpwr (no CVE alias published at this time) |
| **Severity** | High |
| **Summary** | Denial of service via unbounded memory consumption in `Client.list()`. A malicious or compromised FTP server can send an extremely large or never-ending directory listing response, causing the client to accumulate data in an in-memory `StringWriter` buffer without limit, leading to process instability or crash. |
| **Affected versions** | All versions prior to 5.3.0 (last known affected: ≤ 5.2.2) |
| **Fixed version** | 5.3.0 |
| **References** | [GHSA-rp42-5vxx-qpwr](https://github.com/patrickjuchli/basic-ftp/security/advisories/GHSA-rp42-5vxx-qpwr) |

---

## 3. Posture / Recommendation Tweaks

### postcss
- **Minimum safe version** should be updated from **8.4.31** → **8.5.10**.
- **Recommendation note**: The XSS is only exploitable when user-controlled CSS is parsed and then re-stringified for direct embedding inside HTML `<style>` blocks. Projects that do not embed re-stringified user CSS into HTML are not directly affected, but upgrading is still advised as a defense-in-depth measure given the library's massive install base (~216M weekly downloads).

### basic-ftp
- **Minimum safe version** should be updated from **5.2.2** → **5.3.0**.
- **Recommendation note**: The DoS requires connecting to a malicious or compromised FTP server. Consumers that only connect to trusted, controlled servers have lower (but non-zero) exposure. Upgrading to 5.3.0 is recommended regardless, as the fix introduces bounded listing handling.

---

## 4. debug — Should Remain Unchanged

**Rationale**: The existing KB page already captures the compromised-release incident under GHSA-4x49-vf9v-38px / CVE-2025-59144. The additional identifiers surfaced by OSV (MAL-2025-46974, GHSA-8mgj-vmr8-frr6) are confirmed aliases of the same incident — same root cause, same affected version range, same remediation. Adding alias IDs would be a cosmetic normalization only, with no substantive change to guidance, severity, affected range, or recommended action. **No update is warranted.**
